examples.title: Binary Erosion Implementation
examples.description: Pseudocode for binary image erosion using structuring element
examples.code:
FUNCTION BinaryErosion(A, B)
 // A: binary image (2D array of 0/1)
 // B: structuring element (2D array with origin)
 rows ← A.height
 cols ← A.width
 result ← CreateBinaryImage(rows, cols)
 
 FOR y FROM 0 TO rows-1 DO
 FOR x FROM 0 TO cols-1 DO
 contained ← TRUE
 FOR EACH (dy, dx) IN B DO
 bx ← x + dx
 by ← y + dy
 IF bx < 0 OR bx ≥ cols OR by < 0 OR by ≥ rows THEN
 contained ← FALSE
 BREAK
 ELSE IF A[by][bx] = 0 THEN
 contained ← FALSE
 BREAK
 END IF
 END FOR
 result[y][x] ← 1 IF contained = TRUE ELSE 0
 END FOR
 END FOR
 RETURN result
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This function checks if the structuring element B, when placed at each pixel position, is completely contained within the foreground (1s) of image A. If fully contained, that pixel in result becomes 1, otherwise 0. This is the set-theoretic definition of erosion.

examples.title: Binary Dilation Implementation
examples.description: Pseudocode for binary image dilation using structuring element
examples.code:
FUNCTION BinaryDilation(A, B)
 // A: binary image (2D array of 0/1)
 // B: structuring element (2D array with origin)
 rows ← A.height
 cols ← A.width
 result ← CreateBinaryImage(rows, cols)
 
 // Initialize result as copy of A
 result ← CopyImage(A)
 
 FOR y FROM 0 TO rows-1 DO
 FOR x FROM 0 TO cols-1 DO
 IF A[y][x] = 1 THEN // Foreground pixel
 // Place SE at this position and mark all covered pixels
 FOR EACH (dy, dx) IN B DO
 bx ← x + dx
 by ← y + dy
 IF bx ≥ 0 AND bx < cols AND by ≥ 0 AND by < rows THEN
 result[by][bx] ← 1
 END IF
 END FOR
 END IF
 END FOR
 END FOR
 RETURN result
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This function dilates the image by placing the structuring element at every foreground pixel and marking all positions it covers as foreground. This expands the object by the shape and size of the SE.

examples.title: Structuring Element Reflection
examples.description: Pseudocode to compute reflection of a structuring element (180° rotation about origin)
examples.code:
FUNCTION ReflectSE(B)
 // B: structuring element (2D array with origin at center)
 rows ← B.height
 cols ← B.width
 
 reflected ← CreateArray(rows, cols)
 originY ← rows / 2
 originX ← cols / 2
 
 FOR y FROM 0 TO rows-1 DO
 FOR x FROM 0 TO cols-1 DO
 IF B[y][x] = 1 THEN
 // Calculate reflected position
 // Reflection: (x,y) → (-x,-y) relative to origin
 newY - (y - originY)
 newX ← originX - (x - originX)
 reflected[newY][newX] ←Y ← origin 1
 END IF
 END FOR
 END FOR
 RETURN reflected
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: The reflection operation rotates the structuring element by 180° about its origin. This is used in morphological operations like opening, closing, and the hit-or-miss transform. For a symmetric SE (like square or disk), reflection produces the same shape.
